name: eager-l3
description: RAG eager L3 extraction Canary suite. Started as a partial port of `Qualia/scripts/smoke-eager-l3.mjs` (Phase M1, 2026-05-25). The Move-2 Canary `Reload` action unblocked the reload-dependent fixtures; Move 3 (2026-05-27) added the `progress-badge` fixture + the persona gate; Move 4 (2026-05-27) closed Phase 2 v2 with the `provider-swap` fixture. Order is cheapest-first: `reload-smoke` (verifies Reload preserves localStorage + re-establishes hooks) -> `no-provider-noop` (silent-no-op path per ADR 0031) -> `progress-badge` (asserts the EagerExtractionProgressBadge becomes visible during a live sweep; ~10s) -> `cold-launch` (sweep enqueues N > 0 with Ollama provider seeded; ~95s) -> `warm-launch` (idempotency via cache-skip + skip-on-empty-derived; ~30s) -> `provider-swap` (configures Ollama, one extraction lands, swaps to OpenAI-compat-pointed-at-Ollama via `__qualiaSetExtractionProvider`, reloads, asserts the dev-test cache report shows both `ollama=N` AND `openai-compat=M` in the provider breakdown; ~150s). The cold/warm pair has an intentional dependency: cold-launch populates the on-disk behavior cache at `examples/sample/.qualia.rag/behavior-cache.json` which warm-launch's skip-detection relies on. Sweep-dependent fixtures explicitly enable the `compute.rag.eager-l3` persona via `__canarySetPersonaEnabled` before the Reload — required after Move 3 added the persona gate.
